originalTitle: How Roomba started a robot revolution
summary: If you had a Roomba, especially in the early days of the robot vacuum, it was in many ways a fairly unsophisticated machine. It would just bump around your house, looking for something to suck up, until its battery died or its (way too small) tank filled up. Not that it mattered, though. You probably […]
content: If you had a Roomba, especially in the early days of the robot vacuum, it was in many ways a fairly unsophisticated machine. It would just bump around your house, looking for something to suck up, until its battery died or its (way too small) tank filled up. Not that it mattered, though. You probably loved your Roomba. You probably gave it a name. On this episode of Version History, we tell the story of the Roomba, and how it made vacuums lovable. The Verge’s David Pierce and Jennifer Pattison Tuohy are joined by iRobot’s co-founder and former CEO Colin Angle to trace the robovac revolution back to its origins, with a group of engineers trying desperately to build a business out of robots. Any kind of robots. We explain how iRobot developed Roomba for nearly a decade, all the times it nearly fell apart, and the somewhat surprising reasons the product took off. We also look at the vast market that grew around Roomba, and the ways iRobot failed to keep up. This is the second episode of the fourth season of Version History. If you missed last week’s season premiere, on the Harmony remote, make sure you catch up! Here’s how to get every episode, and all our other fun stuff, as soon as it drops: * The Version History podcast feed * The Version History YouTube channel * Our TikTok and Instagram accounts If you’re a Verge subscriber, you can also get access to Version History (and all our other podcasts) with no ads. All you have to do is visit your account settings. If you want to know more about the Roomba’s long backstory, here are a few links to get you started: * How Roomba invented the home robot — and lost the future * From New York: Roomba’s Long, Bumpy Path From Prototype to Your Living Room * From New York: Why the Roomba Company Stopped Making Bots for the Military * From Tech Republic: Joe Jones: Roomba inventor. Roboticist. Vindicated pioneer. * From IEEE Spectrum: iRobot Roomba History: How a Focus Group Changed It - IEEE Spectrum
chineseTitle: Roomba 如何开启机器人革命
chineseSummary: 如果您有一台 Roomba，尤其是在扫地机器人的早期，它在很多方面都只是一台相当简单的机器。它会在你的房子周围颠簸，寻找可以吸收的东西，直到它的电池耗尽或它的（太小）水箱充满了。不过，这并不重要。你可能 [...]
chineseContent: 如果您有一台 Roomba，尤其是在扫地机器人的早期，它在很多方面都只是一台相当简单的机器。它会在你的房子周围颠簸，寻找可以吸收的东西，直到它的电池耗尽或它的（太小）水箱充满了。不过，这并不重要。您可能喜欢您的 Roomba。您可能给它起了一个名字。在本期版本历史中，我们将讲述 Roomba 的故事，以及它如何让吸尘器变得可爱。 The Verge 的 David Pierce 和 Jennifer Pattison Tuohy 与 iRobot 的联合创始人兼前首席执行官 Colin Angle 一起追溯了 robovac 革命的起源，一群工程师拼命地试图用机器人建立一项业务。任何种类的机器人。我们解释了 iRobot 如何在近十年的时间里开发 Roomba，每次都差点崩溃，以及该产品成功的一些令人惊讶的原因。我们还研究了 Roomba 周围不断增长的广阔市场，以及 iRobot 未能跟上的方式。这是版本历史第四季的第二集。如果您错过了上周的季首播，请务必使用 Harmony 遥控器赶上！以下是如何获取每一集以及我们所有其他有趣的内容，一旦发布： * 版本历史记录播客源 * 版本历史记录 YouTube 频道 * 我们的 TikTok 和 Instagram 帐户 如果您是 Verge 订阅者，您还可以访问版本历史记录（以及我们所有其他播客），没有广告。您所要做的就是访问您的帐户设置。如果您想了解更多关于 Roomba 的悠久背景故事，可以通过以下几个链接开始： * Roomba 如何发明家庭机器人并失去未来 * 来自纽约：Roomba 从原型到客厅的漫长而崎岖的道路 * 来自纽约：为什么 Roomba 公司停止了
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
